name: django-formtools

metadata.name: django-formtools
metadata.summary: A set of high-level abstractions for Django forms

documentation:
# django-formtools

```{image} https://jazzband.co/static/img/badge.svg
:alt: Jazzband
:target: https://jazzband.co/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/django-formtools.svg
:alt: PyPI version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/django-formtools/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/django-formtools.svg
:alt: Supported Python versions
:target: https://pypi.org/project/django-formtools/
```

```{image} https://github.com/jazzband/django-formtools/workflows/Test/badge.svg
:alt: GitHub Actions
:target: https://github.com/jazzband/django-formtools/actions
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/jazzband/django-formtools/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:alt: Test Coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/jazzband/django-formtools
```

Django's "formtools" is a set of high-level abstractions for Django forms.
Currently for form previews and multi-step forms.

This code used to live in Django proper -- in `django.contrib.formtools`
-- but was separated into a standalone package in Django 1.8 to keep the
framework's core clean.

For a full list of available formtools, see
<https://django-formtools.readthedocs.io/>

django-formtools can also be found on and installed from the Python
Package Index: <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/django-formtools>

To get more help:

- Join the #django channel on irc.libera.chat. Lots of helpful people hang out
  there.
- Join the django-users mailing list, or read the archives, at
  <https://groups.google.com/group/django-users>.

## Contributing to django-formtools

See `CONTRIBUTING.rst` for information about contributing patches to
`django-formtools`.

Running tests is as simple as [installing Tox](https://tox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html) and running it in the root
Git clone directory:

```
$ git clone https://github.com/jazzband/django-formtools
[..]
$ cd django-formtools
$ tox
[..]
  congratulations :)
```

The previous command will run the tests in different combinations of Python
(if available) and Django versions. To see the full list of combinations use
the `-l` option:

```
$ tox -l
...
py310-djangoAB
py310-djangomain
```

You can run each environment with the `-e` option:

```
$ tox -e py310-djangoAB  # runs the tests only on Python 3.10 and Django A.B.x
```

methods.name: tests.wizard.test_forms.FormTests.test_goto_step_kwargs
documentation: Any extra kwarg given to render_goto_step is added to response context.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.forms.ManagementForm

class_variables.name: formtools.wizard.forms.ManagementForm.template_name
documentation: Remove when Django 5.0 is minimal version.
``ManagementForm`` is used to keep track of the current wizard step.

functions.name: formtools.utils.form_hmac
documentation: Calculates a security hash for the given Form instance.

modules.name: formtools.models
documentation: This file is required to pretend formtools has models.
Otherwise test models cannot be registered.

modules.name: formtools.preview
documentation: Formtools Preview application.

variables.name: formtools.preview.AUTO_ID
documentation: Each form here uses this as its auto_id parameter.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.__init__
documentation: METHODS SUBCLASSES SHOULDN'T OVERRIDE ###################################

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.unused_name
documentation:
Given a first-choice name, adds an underscore to the name until it
reaches a name that isn't claimed by any field in the form.

This is calculated rather than being hard-coded so that no field names
are off-limits for use in the form.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.preview_post
documentation:
Validates the POST data. If valid, displays the preview page.
Else, redisplays form.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.post_post
documentation: Validates the POST data. If valid, calls done(). Else, redisplays form.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.get_auto_id
documentation:
Hook to override the `auto_id` kwarg for the form. Needed when
rendering two form previews in the same template.
METHODS SUBCLASSES MIGHT OVERRIDE IF APPROPRIATE ########################

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.get_initial
documentation:
Takes a request argument and returns a dictionary to pass to the form's
`initial` kwarg when the form is being created from an HTTP get.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.parse_params
documentation:
Given captured args and kwargs from the URLconf, saves something in
self.state and/or raises {class}`~django.http.Http404` if necessary.

For example, this URLconf captures a user_id variable:

```
path('contact/<int:user_id>/', MyFormPreview(MyForm)),
```

In this case, the kwargs variable in parse_params would be
`{'user_id': 32}` for a request to `'/contact/32/'`. You can use
that `user_id` to make sure it's a valid user and/or save it for
later, for use in {meth}`~formtools.preview.FormPreview.done()`.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.process_preview
documentation:
Given a validated form, performs any extra processing before displaying
the preview page, and saves any extra data in context.

By default, this method is empty.  It is called after the form is
validated, but before the context is modified with hash information
and rendered.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.security_hash
documentation:
Calculates the security hash for the given
{class}`~django.http.HttpRequest` and {class}`~django.forms.Form`
instances.

Subclasses may want to take into account request-specific information,
such as the IP address.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.failed_hash
documentation:
Returns an {class}`~django.http.HttpResponse` in the case of
an invalid security hash.

methods.name: formtools.preview.FormPreview.done
documentation:
Does something with the `cleaned_data` data and then needs to
return an {class}`~django.http.HttpResponseRedirect`, e.g. to a
success page.
METHODS SUBCLASSES MUST OVERRIDE ########################################

functions.name: formtools.wizard.views.normalize_name
documentation:
Converts camel-case style names into underscore separated words. Example:

```
>>> normalize_name('oneTwoThree')
'one_two_three'
>>> normalize_name('FourFiveSix')
'four_five_six'
```

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.StepsHelper.current
documentation:
Returns the current step. If no current step is stored in the
storage backend, the first step will be returned.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.as_view
documentation:
This method is used within urls.py to create unique wizardview
instances for every request. We need to override this method because
we add some kwargs which are needed to make the wizardview usable.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_initkwargs
documentation:
Creates a dict with all needed parameters for the form wizard instances

- `form_list` - is a list of forms. The list entries can be single form
  classes or tuples of (`step_name`, `form_class`). If you pass a list
  of forms, the wizardview will convert the class list to
  (`zero_based_counter`, `form_class`). This is needed to access the
  form for a specific step.
- `initial_dict` - contains a dictionary of initial data dictionaries.
  The key should be equal to the `step_name` in the `form_list` (or
  the str of the zero based counter - if no step_names added in the
  `form_list`)
- `instance_dict` - contains a dictionary whose values are model
  instances if the step is based on a `ModelForm` and querysets if
  the step is based on a `ModelFormSet`. The key should be equal to
  the `step_name` in the `form_list`. Same rules as for `initial_dict`
  apply.
- `condition_dict` - contains a dictionary of boolean values or
  callables. If the value of for a specific `step_name` is callable it
  will be called with the wizardview instance as the only argument.
  If the return value is true, the step's form will be used.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_list
documentation:
This method returns a form_list based on the initial form list but
checks if there is a condition method/value in the condition_list.
If an entry exists in the condition list, it will call/read the value
and respect the result. (True means add the form, False means ignore
the form)

The form_list is always generated on the fly because condition methods
could use data from other (maybe previous forms).

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.dispatch
documentation:
This method gets called by the routing engine. The first argument is
`request` which contains a `HttpRequest` instance.
The request is stored in `self.request` for later use. The storage
instance is stored in `self.storage`.

After processing the request using the `dispatch` method, the
response gets updated by the storage engine (for example add cookies).

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get
documentation:
This method handles GET requests.

If a GET request reaches this point, the wizard assumes that the user
just starts at the first step or wants to restart the process.
The data of the wizard will be reset before rendering the first step

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.post
documentation:
This method handles POST requests.

The wizard will render either the current step (if form validation
wasn't successful), the next step (if the current step was stored
successful) or the done view (if no more steps are available)

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.render_next_step
documentation:
This method gets called when the next step/form should be rendered.
`form` contains the last/current form.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.render_goto_step
documentation:
This method gets called when the current step has to be changed.
`goto_step` contains the requested step to go to.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.render_done
documentation:
This method gets called when all forms passed. The method should also
re-validate all steps to prevent manipulation. If any form fails to
validate, `render_revalidation_failure` should get called.
If everything is fine call `done`.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_prefix
documentation:
Returns the prefix which will be used when calling the actual form for
the given step. `step` contains the step-name, `form` the form which
will be called with the returned prefix.

If no step is given, the form_prefix will determine the current step
automatically.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_initial
documentation:
Returns a dictionary which will be passed to the form for `step`
as `initial`. If no initial data was provided while initializing the
form wizard, an empty dictionary will be returned.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_instance
documentation:
Returns an object which will be passed to the form for `step`
as `instance`. If no instance object was provided while initializing
the form wizard, None will be returned.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_kwargs
documentation:
Returns the keyword arguments for instantiating the form
(or formset) on the given step.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form
documentation:
Constructs the form for a given `step`. If no `step` is defined, the
current step will be determined automatically.

The form will be initialized using the `data` argument to prefill the
new form. If needed, instance or queryset (for `ModelForm` or
`ModelFormSet`) will be added too.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.process_step
documentation:
This method is used to postprocess the form data. By default, it
returns the raw `form.data` dictionary.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.process_step_files
documentation:
This method is used to postprocess the form files. By default, it
returns the raw `form.files` dictionary.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.render_revalidation_failure
documentation:
Gets called when a form doesn't validate when rendering the done
view. By default, it changes the current step to failing forms step
and renders the form.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_step_data
documentation:
Is used to return the raw form data. You may use this method to
manipulate the data.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_form_step_files
documentation:
Is used to return the raw form files. You may use this method to
manipulate the data.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_all_cleaned_data
documentation:
Returns a merged dictionary of all step cleaned_data dictionaries.
If a step contains a `FormSet`, the key will be prefixed with
'formset-' and contain a list of the formset cleaned_data dictionaries.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_cleaned_data_for_step
documentation:
Returns the cleaned data for a given `step`. Before returning the
cleaned data, the stored values are revalidated through the form.
If the data doesn't validate, None will be returned.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_next_step
documentation:
Returns the next step after the given `step`. If no more steps are
available, None will be returned. If the `step` argument is None, the
current step will be determined automatically.
If the `step` is not in the current list of steps, the first step will
be returned.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_prev_step
documentation:
Returns the previous step before the given `step`. If there are no
steps available, None will be returned. If the `step` argument is
None, the current step will be determined automatically.
If the `step` is not in the current list of steps, None will be
returned.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_step_index
documentation:
Returns the index for the given `step` name. If no step is given,
the current step will be used to get the index.
If the current step is not in the current list of steps, None will be
returned.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.get_context_data
documentation:
Returns the template context for a step. You can overwrite this method
to add more data for all or some steps. This method returns a
dictionary containing the rendered form step. Available template
context variables are:

> - all extra data stored in the storage backend
> - `wizard` - a dictionary representation of the wizard instance

Example:

```
class MyWizard(WizardView):
    def get_context_data(self, form, **kwargs):
        context = super().get_context_data(form=form, **kwargs)
        if self.steps.current == 'my_step_name':
            context.update({'another_var': True})
        return context
```

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.render
documentation: Returns a `HttpResponse` containing all needed context data.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.WizardView.done
documentation:
This method must be overridden by a subclass to process to form data
after processing all steps.

The WizardView is used to create multi-page forms and handles all the
storage and validation stuff. The wizard is based on Django's generic
class based views.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.views.SessionWizardView
documentation: A WizardView with pre-configured SessionStorage backend.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.views.CookieWizardView
documentation: A WizardView with pre-configured CookieStorage backend.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.get_initkwargs
documentation:
We require a url_name to reverse URLs later. Additionally users can
pass a done_step_name to change the URL name of the "done" view.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.get
documentation: This renders the form or, if needed, does the http redirects.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.post
documentation:
Do a redirect if user presses the prev. step button. The rest of this
is super'd from WizardView.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.get_context_data
documentation:
NamedUrlWizardView provides the url_name of this wizard in the context
dict `wizard`.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.render_next_step
documentation:
When using the NamedUrlWizardView, we have to redirect to update the
browser's URL to match the shown step.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.render_goto_step
documentation:
This method gets called when the current step has to be changed.
`goto_step` contains the requested step to go to.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.render_revalidation_failure
documentation:
When a step fails, we have to redirect the user to the first failing
step.

methods.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlWizardView.render_done
documentation:
When rendering the done view, we have to redirect first (if the URL
name doesn't fit).
A WizardView with URL named steps support.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlSessionWizardView
documentation: A NamedUrlWizardView with pre-configured SessionStorage backend.

classes.name: formtools.wizard.views.NamedUrlCookieWizardView
documentation: A NamedUrlFormWizard with pre-configured CookieStorageBackend.
